Just little notes that say things like “It’s okay to have ups and downs” or “Be kind to yourself.” That way, when I see them, it might help me pause and remember to be gentle with myself in those moments. I could also set a specific time each week to reflect on my progress and check in with how I’m feeling, almost like a little self-care appointment. But then again, I worry that I might forget to do those things or that they won’t be enough to really shift my mindset when I’m in the thick of it. It’s just frustrating because I want to make those reminders stick, but it feels like there’s always something pulling me back into that critical space. I just wish I could find a way to make it feel more automatic, you know?

transcript.speaker: therapist
transcript.content: Therapist: It sounds like placing reminders in visible spots and scheduling a weekly check-in could be helpful strategies for you to reinforce that kindness and self-compassion. I hear that there’s still a concern about forgetting those practices or feeling like they might not be enough when you’re overwhelmed. When you imagine those moments of feeling pulled back into that critical space, what do you think might help you pause and reconnect with those reminders or practices?

transcript.speaker: client
transcript.content: Jessa: You know, I think it might help if I had a specific phrase or mantra that I could repeat to myself in those moments. Something simple that really resonates, like “I’m doing my best” or “It’s okay to feel this way.” If I could anchor myself to that phrase when I start to feel overwhelmed, maybe it would serve as a little lifeline to pull me back to that kinder mindset. I could also try to take a few deep breaths or even step away for a moment, just to create a little space before I dive back into those critical thoughts. But then again, I worry that in the heat of the moment, I might forget to do that or that it won’t feel genuine. It’s just frustrating because I want to be able to access that kindness more easily, but it feels like there’s always this barrier in the way. I just wish I could find a way to make those practices feel more instinctive, you know?
